NEXT:         🎯 Item 8 — Initial Site Admin assignment in tenant create
              flow (PRODUCT.md §210 bullet 3 "Assign initial Site Admin
              per tenant").

              CONTEXT: Today's apps/web/src/app/admin/tenants/create-tenant-dialog.tsx
              (135L) creates a tenant via trpc.platform.create.useMutation
              with only {name, slug, timezone, currency}. No first-user
              flow. After Item 7 ship, platformUser.create exists and is
              UI-consumed at /admin/users — but new tenants land with
              zero users + no UI hint about needing one. Item 8 closes
              this gap so a fresh tenant has a sign-in-able operator
              from day one.

              SCOPE (~270L total, Tier 1 single Sonnet dispatch):

              Approach A (PREFERRED — atomic procedure):
                Files to CREATE/MODIFY (~270L):
                  apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/platform.ts (M, ~+85L)
                    Add new procedure: createTenantWithAdmin
                      Input: tenant fields ({name, slug, timezone, currency})
                             + admin fields ({email, fullName, password,
                               languagePreference})
                      Logic: prisma.$transaction([
                               platformPrisma.tenant.create({ data: tenantInput }),
                               then on tenant.id: platformPrisma.user.create({
                                 data: { ...adminInput, role: 'site_admin',
                                   tenantId: tenant.id,
                                   passwordHash: bcrypt.hash(password, 12),
                                   isActive: true }
                               })
                             ])
                      writeAuditLog for both creates.
                      Throws BAD_REQUEST on tenant.slug collision (existing
                      tenant.create handles this — keep parity).
                  apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/platform.test.ts (M, +60L)
                    3 new cases under platform.createTenantWithAdmin describe:
                      * creates tenant + site_admin user atomically
                      * rolls back tenant on user-creation failure (mock
                        user.create to throw, assert tenant.create rolled back)
                      * hashes password before storage (assert bcrypt called)
                  apps/web/src/app/admin/tenants/create-tenant-dialog.tsx (M, ~+90L)
                    Add new collapsed-by-default <Collapsible> section:
                    "Initial Site Admin (recommended)" with email + fullName
                    + password (Input type=password, minLength 12) +
                    languagePreference select.
                    If section opened + any field filled: ALL admin fields
                    become required. If section collapsed/empty: ship as
                    today (tenant-only via existing platform.create).
                    Route switch:
                      - admin section filled → trpc.platform.createTenantWithAdmin
                      - admin section empty/closed → trpc.platform.create
                    onSuccess: invalidate platform.list + platformUser.list
                    + platform.metrics. handleClose resets both sections.
                  apps/web/src/app/admin/tenants/__tests__/admin-tenants-client.test.tsx (M, +35L)
                    2 new cases:
                      * dialog with admin section filled calls
                        createTenantWithAdmin with combined payload
                      * dialog with admin section closed calls
                        platform.create (current behaviour preserved)

              Approach B (FALLBACK — chain, less atomic):
                Use existing tenant.create + platformUser.create back-to-back
                in the client. Worse: orphan tenant if user creation fails.
                Only switch to B if A's atomic transaction faces a Prisma
                schema constraint that blocks the nested create.

              DECOMPOSITION: Tier 1 (single dispatch).
                wc -l totals: platform.ts 281L modify ~85 + tests 60 + dialog
